content:
# Branch-First Workflow -- Instruction (2026-03-28)

All development work must happen on feature branches and be merged via pull requests. Never commit directly to master. No exceptions, regardless of change size.

This applies to: follow-up fixes, test-only changes, single-file edits, documentation, knowledge store syncs in the code repo. Everything.

## Process
1. Create branch from master (e.g. `fix/flaky-tests`, `feat/42-cold-tier`)
2. Commit on the branch
3. Push and create PR via `gh pr create`
4. Merge to master
5. Delete the branch

## Why
Follow-up commits for issue #42 (cold tier test, flaky HNSW fixes) were pushed directly to master without PRs, bypassing review and traceability. Owner reinforced that branch-first is mandatory for all changes.
